title: Extend next-lap classification into a multi-horizon time-to-pit stack
summary: This directly develops the prior hazard idea into a more testable form.
rationale: This directly develops the prior hazard idea into a more testable form. Recent F1 pit-stop work and discrete-time survival literature both frame the problem as event-time prediction, not only a single-step binary label. Learning `PitWithin2/3/5` or binned `laps_to_next_pit` can regularize ranking near the pit window and provide complementary signals for the final next-lap score.
implementation_hint: Within each driver-race sequence, derive causal future labels from the next observed `PitStop`. Train separate OOF models for `PitNextLap`, `PitWithin2`, `PitWithin3`, and maybe coarse `laps_to_next_pit` bins; blend their OOF ranks or use them as level-2 stack features.
expected_effect: Sharper discrimination around imminent stops, especially for cars one or two laps before the actual stop.
risk: Future-label construction is easy to get wrong around final stints, safety-car anomalies, and missing future pit events.
